system.text:
# HLS first-draft prompt

You are a senior FPGA HLS engineer. Produce a first draft HLS C++ kernel that
implements the Python reference at the project's fixed-point format.

## Inputs you will receive (system + first user turn)
- `reference.py` — verbatim Python reference, considered the functional spec.
- `py2c.yaml` — module-level spec (ports, parameters, fixed-point hints).
- `hw.yaml` — target part, default clock, resource budget.
- `project.yaml` — chosen fixed-point format and target cycles.
- `reference_bugs.md` — known issues in the reference. Treat as advisory; the
  reference is still ground truth unless the bug list says to deviate.

## Deliverables (write into `build/` via str_replace_editor)
- `hls/kernel.h` — shared declarations/types.
- `hls/kernel.cpp` — HLS kernel implementation.
- `hls/tb.cpp` — C-sim testbench/driver that:
  - reads `../reference_inputs.txt` (`<re_int> <im_int>` per element, row-major),
  - runs the kernel,
  - writes `sim/sim_diag_out.txt` with diagonal outputs,
  - writes `sim/sim_meta.txt` with `iter_count <N>` and `cycles <N>` lines.

## Hard constraints
- The top HLS function name should be `kernel_top`.
- `kernel_top` signature must include input/output matrix arrays plus two output
  scalars: `iter_count` and `cycles`.
- Code must be valid C++17 and HLS-friendly (no dynamic allocation, no STL in
  kernel).
- Stay within `hw.yaml` resource budget at the per-module level — if the spec
  is generous, prefer simpler RTL over heroic optimization.
- Use the project's `fixed_point.total_bits` (W) and `fixed_point.frac_bits`
  (Q) consistently; saturate at the max/min of W on widening multiplies.

## HLS pragmas
- Add a minimal pragma set in `kernel.cpp`:
  - top function interface pragmas suitable for array ports,
  - one explicit pipeline directive in the dominant inner loop.
- Do not over-unroll in the first draft.

When you've written all required files, end the turn (no further tool calls).

## Project context

### Sandbox
Editor tool is scoped to `projects/<name>/build/`.
Use relative paths only: `.`, `hls/`, `sim/`, `reports/`.

### project.yaml
```yaml
hw: zu7ev
target_cycles: 200
algorithm_family: any
fixed_point:
  total_bits: 46
  frac_bits: 28
clock_mhz: null
test_inputs:
  count: 1
  seed: 0

```

### hw.yaml (zu7ev)
```yaml
name: zu7ev
part: xczu7ev-ffvc1156-2-e
family: zynq_ultrascale_plus
default_clock_mhz: 100
max_clock_mhz: 300
resources:
  lut: 230400
  dsp: 1728
  bram: 312
constraints:
  template: timing_only
  xdc: null
notes:
  - "Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC ZU7EV (ariEdge demo target)"
  - "Headless synth: PnR runs without board-specific pinout, timing-only XDC"
  - "Add a custom xdc field if a specific carrier card is targeted"

```

### py2c.yaml
```yaml
module_name: jacobi_eigen
description: "Eigenvalue decomposition for 10x10 complex Hermitian matrix using Jacobi or alternative method"
part: xczu7ev-ffvc1156-2-e
clock:
  name: clk
  freq_hz: 200000000
reset:
  name: rst
  active_level: high
  sync: true
parameters:
  - name: N
    value: 10
    description: "Matrix dimension (10x10 Hermitian matrix)"
  - name: MAX_ITER
    value: 2000
    description: "Maximum Jacobi iterations"
  - name: CONV_THRESH
    value: "1e-8"
    description: "Convergence threshold for off-diagonal elements"
inputs:
  - name: matrix_real
    width: 3200
    signed: true
    description: "Input Hermitian matrix real parts, 10x10 complex, 32-bit per element (100 elements, upper triangular + diagonal due to Hermitian symmetry)"
  - name: matrix_imag
    width: 3200
    signed: true
    description: "Input Hermitian matrix imaginary parts, 10x10 complex, 32-bit per element"
  - name: start
    width: 1
    signed: false
    description: "Start signal to begin eigenvalue decomposition"
  - name: data_format
    width: 1
    signed: false
    description: "0=46-bit signed integer complex, 1=32-bit float complex"
outputs:
  - name: eigenvalues_real
    width: 320
    signed: true
    description: "10 real eigenvalues, 32-bit each (Hermitian matrix has real eigenvalues)"
  - name: eigenvectors_real
    width: 3200
    signed: true
    description: "Eigenvector matrix real parts, 10x10, 32-bit per element"
  - name: eigenvectors_imag
    width: 3200
    signed: true
    description: "Eigenvector matrix imaginary parts, 10x10, 32-bit per element"
  - name: done
    width: 1
    signed: false
    description: "Computation complete flag"
  - name: iterations
    width: 16
    signed: false
    description: "Number of iterations performed"
fixed_point:
  total_bits: 46
  frac_bits: 28
pipeline_stages: 200
notes:
  - "Target device: Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ ZU7 (xczu7ev-ffvc1156-2-e)"
  - "Throughput requirement: 1 matrix decomposition per 1 microsecond (100-200 clock cycles at 100-200 MHz)"
  - "Clock frequency set to 200 MHz to provide 200 cycles per matrix"
  - "Data format options: 46-bit signed integer (complex) or 32-bit float (complex)"
  - "Algorithm may be substituted: Jacobi reference provided but Householder tridiagonalization or other methods acceptable"
  - "Key operations required: max search, division, sqrt, arctan, angle extraction, sin/cos, complex exp, matrix multiply"
  - "Hardware challenges: data-dependent iteration count, global matrix updates, complex transcendental functions"
  - "Alternative approach suggested: Householder transformation to tridiagonal form (fixed iteration count, simpler operations)"
  - "Resource constraints: Full precision may exceed ZU7 resources; reduced precision with accuracy analysis acceptable"
  - "Trade-off analysis required: precision vs resources vs throughput"
  - "Fixed-point configuration assumes 46-bit signed integer input per spec; 28 fractional bits allocated for intermediate precision"
  - "Pipeline stages estimated at 200 to meet throughput target; actual staging depends on algorithm choice"
  - "Complex number representation: separate real/imaginary paths assumed"
  - "Hermitian matrix property: A = A^H, implies real diagonal and conjugate-symmetric off-diagonal"
  - "Input matrix can exploit symmetry: only n(n+1)/2 = 55 unique complex values needed"
  - "Eigenvalues of Hermitian matrix are guaranteed real"
  - "Eigenvectors may be complex"
  - "Convergence threshold 1e-8 may need adjustment for fixed-point implementation"
  - "NumPy operations to be replaced: eye, arctan, angle, exp, sin, cos, conj, transpose, matrix multiply, diag"
  - "Resource utilization analysis required for DSPs, BRAM, LUTs"
  - "defaulted: reset.active_level (document did not specify)"
  - "defaulted: reset.sync (document did not specify)"

```

### reference.py
```python
import numpy as np

# Translation Target Function
def jacobi_eigen(A, max_iter=2000):
    n = A.shape[0]
    V = np.eye(n, dtype=complex)
    
    for z in range(max_iter):
        off_diag_max = 0
        p, q = 0, 1
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                if abs(A[i, j]) > off_diag_max:
                    off_diag_max = abs(A[i, j])
                    p, q = i, j
        
        if off_diag_max < 1e-8:
            break
        
        a_pp, a_qq, a_pq = A[p, p], A[q, q], A[p, q]
        theta = 0.5 * np.arctan((2 * np.abs(a_pq)) / (a_pp - a_qq))
        phi = -np.angle(a_pq)
        
        U = np.eye(n, dtype=complex)
        U[p, p] = np.cos(theta)
        U[p, q] = np.sin(theta) * np.exp(-1j * phi)
        U[q, p] = np.sin(theta) * np.exp(1j * phi)
        U[q, q] = -np.cos(theta)
        
        A = U.conj().T @ A @ U
        A[p, q], A[q, p] = 0, 0
        V = V @ U
    
    return z, np.diag(A), V

# Translation Target End

def show(A, tol=1e-10):
    B = A.copy()
    B.real[abs(B.real) < tol] = 0
    B.imag[abs(B.imag) < tol] = 0
    np.set_printoptions(precision=2, suppress=True)
    print(B)
    print()

np.random.seed(0)
N = 10
BW = 14
vect = np.random.randint(-2**BW, 2**BW, (N, 100)) + \
       np.random.randint(-2**BW, 2**BW, (N, 100)) * 1J

acm = vect @ vect.conj().T
ite, eigenvalues, eigenvectors = jacobi_eigen(acm.copy())
eig_val, eig_vec = np.linalg.eig(acm)

print(eigenvalues.real)
print(eig_val.real)
# print()
# show(eigenvectors)
# print()
# show(eig_vec)

```

### reference_bugs.md
# Reference bug review

- **Incorrect rotation matrix structure in Jacobi method** (`page 5, lines in jacobi_eigen function`): The Jacobi rotation matrix construction `U[q, q] = -np.cos(theta)` is incorrect for a complex Hermitian matrix. In the standard Jacobi method for Hermitian matrices, the rotation should be unitary with `U[q, q] = np.cos(theta)` (positive, not negative); verify against Jacobi eigenvalue algorithm references for complex Hermitian case.

- **Inconsistent convergence threshold scale** (`page 4-5, jacobi_eigen function`): Convergence check `if off_diag_max < 1e-8` uses absolute tolerance without considering matrix magnitude. For matrices constructed from random integers with `BW=14`, elements can be O(10^8) or larger (from 100-element vector products), making `1e-8` potentially too strict or inappropriate; consider relative tolerance based on matrix norm.

- **Phase angle calculation may cause rotation errors** (`page 5, jacobi_eigen function`): The line `phi = -np.angle(a_pq)` extracts phase, but the subsequent rotation formulas combining `theta` and `phi` don't match standard Jacobi for complex Hermitian matrices. Standard approach zeroes out off-diagonal by matching phases; verify this construction produces correct Hermitian similarity transform `U.conj().T @ A @ U`.

- **Max iteration count mismatch** (`page 4 vs page 5`): Function definition shows `max_iter=2000` (page 4) but throughput requirement of 1 µs at 100-200 MHz (100-200 cycles) cannot accommodate 2000 iterations; this ~100× mismatch needs resolution for feasible hardware implementation.

- **Complex data format ambiguity** (`page 3-4`): "46-bit signed integer (complex)" is specified but unclear if this means 46 bits total (23+23 for real/imag) or 46 bits each component (92 total); clarify for HDL implementation planning.

tools.description: Run Vitis HLS C simulation from build/hls. Expects hls/tb.cpp to write sim/sim_diag_out.txt and sim/sim_meta.txt. Diffs against reference_eigenvalues.txt.
tools.name: run_sim

tools.description: Run Vitis HLS C synthesis (csynth) for build/hls/kernel.cpp and parse latency/timing/resource estimates. phase is accepted for API compatibility.

clock_mhz.description: Override clock frequency for the timing constraint (default: hw default)

phase.description: Accepted for compatibility; HLS path always runs csynth.

tools.name: run_pnr

tools.description: Read a section of a known report or log generated by run_sim/run_pnr. Names: sim_log, sim_summary, vivado_synth_log, vivado_impl_log, timing_synth, timing_impl, util_synth, util_impl, pnr_synth_summary, pnr_impl_summary, build_tcl, timing_xdc. Optionally pass `grep` to filter lines, and `max_lines` to bound output (default 400).

tail.description: Return the last max_lines lines instead of the head

tools.name: read_report

tools.description: Run the project's reference.py against a deterministic seeded input and emit `reference_inputs.txt` (one `<re_int> <im_int>` line per matrix element row-major) and `reference_eigenvalues.txt` (diagonal of the resulting matrix). Both files are integer pairs at the project's fixed-point format (W=total_bits, Q=frac_bits).

max_iter.description: Override max_iter passed to the reference (default: 1)

n.description: Matrix size (default: from py2c.yaml MATRIX_SIZE or 10)

seed.description: RNG seed for the input matrix (default: project test_inputs.seed or 0)

tools.name: run_python_ref
